scope.notes: Comparative synthesis derived only from the three local Company Brand Audit fixtures and their Browser Evidence request IDs.

comparative_synthesis.summary: Across the local fixture audits, the shared market frame is talent-experience work with different proof centers: Symphony Talent emphasizes craft, impact, technology, and client partnership; Phenom emphasizes intelligent personalization, product pace, customer outcomes, and builder culture; Radancy emphasizes enterprise hiring scale, global reach, established client programs, and specialist recruitment marketing expertise.

shared_themes.summary: All three fixtures connect employee work to improving hiring or talent experiences, but they differ in whether the proof is framed as craft and relevance, product personalization, or enterprise reach.

shared_themes.theme: Professional expertise as the employer promise
shared_themes.summary: Symphony Talent and Radancy explicitly lean on professional craft or specialist expertise, while Phenom translates expertise into product building and intelligent experience creation.

shared_themes.theme: Proof gaps in the local slice
shared_themes.summary: The three company audits all record missing review-site, benefits, employee voice, and concrete career mobility evidence, so the comparative read should stay limited to fixture-level positioning rather than market-current claims.

key_differentiators.summary: The strongest client differentiator in the local evidence is the combination of creative storytelling, recruitment marketing technology, craft, and client partnership in one employer promise.
Phenom is differentiated by a product-led personalization story that positions employees as builders of intelligent talent moments tied to customer outcomes.
Radancy is differentiated by status proof: enterprise hiring programs, global reach, established client work, and specialist recruitment marketing expertise.

whitespace_opportunities.opportunity: Own the human relevance layer between creative craft and recruitment technology.
whitespace_opportunities.rationale: Symphony Talent can use future evidence to make its craft-and-impact promise more concrete against Phenom's product-personalization story and Radancy's scale-and-reputation proof.

whitespace_opportunities.opportunity: Add proof of employee voice, career growth, benefits, and review sentiment before drawing stronger recommendations.
whitespace_opportunities.rationale: Each source company audit flags unavailable review-site evidence and limited fixture scope, leaving lifestyle, sentiment, and concrete mobility proof underdeveloped across the set.

kilos_positioning_matrix.client_read: Symphony Talent frames impact as helping employers reach candidates with more relevant talent experiences through craft, technology, and partnership.

competitor_reads.read: Phenom frames impact as making every talent moment more personal through intelligent product work and customer outcomes.
Radancy's local evidence is less impact-led and more focused on enterprise scale and expertise.

kilos_positioning_matrix.synthesis: Impact is a shared category, but Symphony Talent and Phenom carry clearer impact narratives in this fixture slice.

kilos_positioning_matrix.client_read: Symphony Talent signals opportunity through craft, recruitment marketing technology, and client partnership as expertise-building contexts.

competitor_reads.read: Phenom implies opportunity through builder culture and intelligent product work, but the fixture does not prove specific mobility paths.
Radancy signals opportunity through specialist recruitment marketing expertise and enterprise program exposure.

kilos_positioning_matrix.synthesis: Opportunity is present as expertise and exposure, but none of the local fixtures prove concrete career progression or internal mobility.

kilos_positioning_matrix.client_read: Symphony Talent's local fixture is collaborative in tone but does not provide employee community, belonging, or voice proof.

competitor_reads.read: Phenom has the clearest kinship signal through collaborative problem solving across teams.
Radancy's local fixture does not foreground kinship beyond the implied professional community around specialist expertise.

kilos_positioning_matrix.synthesis: Kinship is the weakest comparative dimension in the local evidence, with Phenom holding the clearest available signal.

kilos_positioning_matrix.client_read: Symphony Talent does not rely on reputation or scale proof in the local hero fixture.

competitor_reads.read: Phenom's local evidence is product-led rather than status-led.
Radancy owns the clearest status position through enterprise hiring programs, global reach, and established client work.

kilos_positioning_matrix.synthesis: Status is Radancy's strongest relative lane in this fixture set.

kilos_positioning_matrix.client_read: Symphony Talent has no benefits, flexibility, work-life, or stability evidence in the local fixture.

competitor_reads.read: Phenom has no lifestyle evidence in the local fixture.
Radancy has no lifestyle evidence in the local fixture.

kilos_positioning_matrix.synthesis: Lifestyle is unavailable across the comparative fixture and should not be inferred from the current evidence.

review_site_comparison.summary: No Glassdoor, Indeed, or other review-site evidence is present in any of the three local Company Brand Audit fixtures.
review_site_comparison.caveat: Review platforms are required in the full workflow but are outside this first local comparative fixture slice.

standout_engagement_examples.example: Hero-level positioning connects employee craft and client partnership to candidate relevance.
Culture-card positioning turns personalization into a product-building employee story.
Proof-block positioning uses enterprise scale and specialist expertise as employer-brand proof.

implications_for_client.implication: Defend the craft-and-impact lane with sharper proof.
implications_for_client.summary: Symphony Talent has a credible craft, technology, and client-partnership position in the fixture, but Phenom can outflank it on product energy and Radancy can outflank it on scale unless future evidence adds employee stories, outcome proof, and clearer career signals.

implications_for_client.implication: Treat lifestyle and review sentiment as unresolved evidence requirements.
implications_for_client.summary: Because all three company audits mark review-site coverage as unavailable and omit benefits or flexibility proof, the comparative audit should not make employee sentiment or lifestyle recommendations beyond identifying the gap.

evidence_backed_claims.claim: The three local company audit fixtures share a talent-experience employer-brand frame while emphasizing different proof centers.
Symphony Talent's local client fixture is most differentiated by the blend of craft, recruitment marketing technology, impact, and client partnership.
Phenom's local competitor fixture is most differentiated by product-led personalization and builder culture.
Radancy's local competitor fixture is most differentiated by enterprise scale, global reach, and specialist recruitment marketing expertise.

caveats: This comparative audit cites only the three local Company Brand Audit fixtures and their Browser Evidence request IDs.
The fixture is not a live-web comparative audit and does not support market-current claims.
Review-site, benefits, employee story, and career-mobility evidence are unavailable in this slice.
The file is read-only provenance/source metadata, not a generated report or viewer payload.
